commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human maintainer review is needed to accept or reject the model-visible historical timestamp compatibility change; no automated code repair is indicated.; Cleared: The diff does not change CI workflows, dependencies, package scripts, credentials, auth, sandboxing, or external code execution paths; no concrete security or supply-chain concern was found. (sha=690cd1b3588d79b85b5c328dd74748fdb6ddb7f9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves direct user-turn timestamping from gateway/TUI ingress to the embedded LLM boundary, canonicalizes text-only user messages for stable serialization, preserves CLI prompt timestamping, stores normalized inbound timestamps, and adds cache-stability tests.

PR surface: Source +360, Tests +585. Total +945 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows direct gateway paths stamp only the current user turn before persistence, while the current LLM boundary does not apply stable per-message stamping; that makes the reported current-vs-history byte flip source-reproducible without executing the full provider path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review is needed to accept or reject the model-visible historical timestamp compatibility change; no automated code repair is indicated.

target.title: fix(agents): stabilize user-turn serialization across turns to preserve prompt cache
target.body:
**AI-assisted PR (Claude).** Marked per CONTRIBUTING; human-run real-behavior proof from a live setup is below.

## Summary

What problem does this PR solve? — The gateway decorates the **current** user message (a `[timestamp]` prefix from [#3658](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/3658) + a content-block wrap) but does **not** persist the decoration. When that message ages into history it reverts to its bare stored string, so its wire bytes change and the provider prompt cache is invalidated from that message to the end of the prompt — on **every** new user turn.

Why now? — On `anthropic-messages` this re-writes the suffix at the **1.25× cache-write premium** every turn and never collects the 0.1× reads, so it costs more than not caching at all. It also hits `openai-completions` and local vLLM/llama.cpp/Ollama (slower prefill). Full analysis: #90810.

Intended outcome — Every user message serializes **byte-identically** whether it is the current turn or history, so the cached prefix is reused across turns.

Out of scope — `openai-chatgpt-responses` (already immune via server-side continuation); the separate `cache_control` mechanisms in [#86063](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86063) and the merged tool-loop fix [#89488](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/89488).

Success looks like — same user message is byte-identical as current turn and as history; `cache_read` tracks the growing prefix while `cache_write` collapses to the new delta.

Reviewer focus — (1) the boundary stamp is deterministic per message and never mutates on aging; (2) content-form canonicalization is applied **only** to text-only turns and does **not** alter attachment/image turns that legitimately need block arrays.

## Linked context

Closes #90810

Related [#86063](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86063) (different mechanism — `cache_control` placement / metadata strip), [#89488](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/89488) (merged, separate slice), [#3658](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/3658) (the feature this regresses from; kept intact).

Requested by a maintainer or owner? — No; filed proactively with the issue above.

## How it works

- [`normalizeMessagesForLlmBoundary`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.ts) now stamps **every** user turn (current and historical) from that message's own stored `timestamp`. Storage stays bare; the stamp is deterministic, so bytes never change as a message ages.
- Text-only user turns canonicalize to a plain-string `content` (matching how history is stored/replayed); attachment/image turns keep their block arrays.
- The four transient `injectTimestamp` call sites (`chat.ts`, `agent.ts`, `tui/embedded-backend.ts`, `server-restart-sentinel.ts`) are removed in favor of the single boundary stamp.

This **keeps [#3658](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/3658) intact** (same prefix-on-the-message format small local models parse reliably) and, as a bonus, the model now sees every message's timestamp — real historical time awareness, not just "now".

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** prompt cache invalidated on every new user turn on full-resend transports.
- **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw 2026.6.1 GA, macOS 15 (Darwin 24.6.0), Node 25.9.0, npm-global install; live traffic on `claude-opus-4-8` over an Anthropic-Messages-compatible provider, plus a transport-level wire capture on an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** built and swapped the patched dist into the live install; ran a normal multi-turn conversation; recorded the per-turn `usage` fields (input / cache_read / cache_write) and cross-checked them token-for-token against the provider's own billing dashboard. Separately captured two consecutive outbound request bodies and diffed the previous user message's serialization.
- **Evidence after fix (copied live output):** copied live output from a ~30-minute multi-turn session: `cache_read` climbed with the conversation **83,300 → 85,789** while `cache_write` was the new delta only (**27–713** tokens; write-share **~26% → ~1.4%**). Reconciliation was **token-exact** vs the provider dashboard on every sampled turn (e.g. read 83,882 + write 470 = 84,352 cached). After a >5-min TTL gap, a single full re-write then reads resumed — impossible under the bug. Wire diff: the previous user message is now **byte-identical** as current turn and as history (before: decorated block `"content":[{…}]` vs bare string `"content":"…"`).
- **Observed result after fix:** prefix reused across turns; per-turn cost dropped from re-write-priced to read-priced.
- **Before evidence:** pre-fix, `cache_read` was pinned at the static system+tools floor (~53,875) with the suffix re-billed as `cache_creation` every turn (worst case ~131k tokens re-written in one tool-heavy turn).
- **What was not tested:** (1) decoration-as-sole-cause is wire-isolated on `openai-completions`; on `anthropic-messages` it is inferred (shared boundary serialization) but not separately wire-captured, because that path also carries `cache_control`. (2) the full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` suite was not run end-to-end in this environment; targeted typechecks + the new regression test were run (below). (3) no `codex review` run.

## Tests and validation

- **Commands run:** `tsgo` core typecheck (clean) and `tsgo` core **test** typecheck (clean) on the rebased branch.
- **Regression coverage added:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.cache-stability.test.ts` — asserts the same user turn serializes byte-identically as current vs historical at the LLM boundary.
- **What failed before this fix:** that new test **fails before** the change (current sent decorated / historical sent bare) and **passes after**. Validated fail-before/pass-after.
- The existing `attempt.llm-boundary` suite was extended to cover the boundary stamp.

## Risk checklist

- **User-visible behavior change?** Yes (minor): every user turn now carries a deterministic per-message `[timestamp]` prefix at the LLM boundary (previously only the current turn). This is the #3658 format, applied consistently.
- **Config / env / migration change?** No.
- **Security / auth / secrets / network / tool-execution change?** No. (Touched files are not under any CODEOWNERS secops pattern.)
- **Highest-risk area:** content-form canonicalization could, if mis-scoped, flatten attachment/image turns that need block arrays.
- **Mitigation:** canonicalization is gated to text-only turns; attachment/image turns keep arrays, covered by the boundary tests.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** maintainer review.
- **Waiting on:** CI; maintainer review. "Allow edits by maintainers" left enabled on the fork branch.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** none yet (new PR).

---

*Investigated and implemented with AI assistance (Claude). cc @Peetiegonzalez*
target.author: marvinthebored
